HvacSystem applies thermal bleed toward outdoor temp + nearest HvacUnit within 10m pushes temp toward target_temp + occupants emit CO2 + vent units swap atmosphere with outside (420 ppm baseline). MedicalSystem applies per-condition health_per_sec * severity * dt each tick (negative = damage from infection, positive = regen from healing), drops conditions when seconds_remaining hits zero, removes empty MedicalConditions to keep the world tidy, exposes apply_condition() helper for FireSystem and AI to inject conditions

🚀 **Deployed** `3cfb289` by Shaostoul: v0.185.x pill width measurement now precise - text and pill no longer overlap. Operator reported timestamp text Þ and message reply text overlapping. Root cause - my pill_width estimate (52px + 22px per reaction) did not match the actual rendered width and the layout-spacer in message_row used pill_width / (body_font * 0.5) for space count which is way off because spaces in proportional fonts are about 3.5px not body_font/2 (=6.5). So I was reserving roughly 30px of layout space when the pill was actually 80px wide - massive overlap. TWO-PART fix. ONE new compute_pill_width helper in chat.rs measures the EXACT rendered width using ctx.fonts().layout_no_wrap on the same strings and font_ids paint_timestamp_pill uses then sums - left_pad(6) + ts_w + gap(5) + thorn_w + (gap_before_first(4) + sum(badge_body) + sum(gap 3 between)) + right_pad(6). chat.rs now passes the precise measurement to message_row instead of the rough estimate. TWO message_row gained a measured-space-width path. Was - n_spaces = pill_width / (body_font * 0.5) which is a wrong constant. Now - measure a single space using ui.fonts().layout_no_wrap then n = ceil(pill_width / space_w). Reservation is exactly pill_width pixels. paint_timestamp_pill spacing math also corrected - the badge advance was off by 1 (was advance += label_w + 3 with badge body = label_w + 4 = badges overlapped each other by 1px). Now badge body = label_w + 4 and post-badge advance is also label_w + 4 with the inter-badge gap added at the START of the next iteration. Both functions now share identical math so pill_width matches the rendered pixels

The egui font has spotty Unicode coverage outside the basic ranges. Rather than guess at more glyphs, use plain text labels Pin and Edit (small font_size_small with channeling color matching the other popup glyphs). Reply stays as ↩ since that arrow does render. Wider min_size on the buttons (34 and 38) to fit the text. The sticky-hover popup width estimate was also updated so the combined region covers the new wider buttons. SECOND issue - the heart reaction badge displayed heart-square-1 instead of heart-1. The square was a U+FE0F variation selector embedded in the OLD reaction key stored in the database (clients before the v0.186 strip stored ❤️ with FE0F now we store the bare ❤). Fix - paint_timestamp_pill now strips FE0F when building the badge LABEL but keeps the original key when sending the toggle click back to the relay so the matching entry actually toggles. compute_pill_width does the same strip when measuring so the reservation matches the rendered badge width. Three places use the chars().filter(|c| *c != FE0F).collect() pattern - paint_timestamp_pill display TOP_REACTIONS popup ALL_REACTIONS popup. Old ❤️1 reactions still in the DB will display correctly post-fix; new reactions go in as bare ❤

🚀 **Deployed** `6d1d258` by Shaostoul: v0.201.0 per-role upload size limits - operator follow-up to v0.200 server settings expansion. Operator caught that v0.200 added per-role char limits (4 fields) but kept upload size as a single value, asking for variable upload size between unverified / verified / mod / admin. Schema migration adds 4 new columns max_upload_mb_unverified / verified / mod / admin to server_settings with trust-ladder defaults (5 / 25 / 100 / 500 MB respectively). The migration also FORWARDS any non-default v0.200 max_upload_mb value into all 4 new columns so an operator who'd already tuned the single value doesn't lose their configuration. Old single max_upload_mb column is RETAINED for v0.200 client backward compat (kept in sync with the unverified value going forward, which is the most-conservative limit). New ServerSettings::max_upload_mb_for_role(role) helper mirrors max_chars_for_role. set_server_settings now writes all 4 new columns + the legacy mirror. ServerSettingsUpdate WS message gains 4 new Optional fields. Handler clamps each to (1..10_000) MB and applies independently. If a v0.200 client sends only the legacy max_upload_mb field, the handler propagates it to all 4 per-role columns so behavior matches the operator's intent. Native UI - read-only mode shows 4 separate kv_rows for the per-role caps. Edit mode replaces the single Max upload form_row with 4 form_rows each labeled by role + default. Save WS payload sends the 4 new fields and OMITS the legacy single field (server's v0.201 handler treats per-role values as authoritative when present). Local exe + relay binary in CI both build clean. cargo check + theme_token_lint + theme_editor_coverage + icon_glyph_lint + 201 relay unit tests all green. Operator's other questions answered in chat (capability-based permissions vs tier-based + Vessel-vs-region distinction with worked examples for reactor/bridge/hangar + alternatives to cubic chunks like octrees / kd-trees / r-trees / Hilbert curves with recommendation for hybrid cubic-chunks-for-rogues + octree-for-systems).
